title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: Status: Implemented (v0.4.2) — Phase 0–1 complete, plus added validation kernel materialisation cache; consumer migration (Phases 2–4) pending · Author: drafted Travis Shepherd · Date drafted: 2026-06-08 · Last reconciled: 2026-06-16 lean, domain-agnostic R package tabular data ingestion: read messy field/lab workbooks, detect layout, map columns onto declared schema, parse non-detects/units, validate result — shared water-chemistry-qaqc (chemistry) bw-analysis-code (fish, effort, habitat, eventually metals).

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: repos independently grew ingestion layer, real overlap real gaps: bw-analysis-code stronger structural design: declarative, role-based data contract (DATA_CONTRACTS = catch/effort/fhap), synonym + fuzzy auto_map(), validate_against_contract(), persisted JSON mapping profiles. non-detect/censored handling chemistry contract — even though ingests tissue-metals sediment workbooks. water-chemistry-qaqc stronger lab-data primitives: --text reading, long/wide layout detection + melt, non-detect parsing (<0.01, ND, separate DL columns), substitution, unit reconciliation. column mapping fixed suggest_roles() keyed one hardcoded wqdata schema — far less general bw’s contract engine. Neither superset. small shared package lets repo use best stops duplication diverging. Non-goal: place domain knowledge — fish biology, guideline tables, wqdata/catch field lists, statistics. stay consuming repos. tritonIngest plumbing .

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: wq: wqdata model + accessors, guideline engine + tables, censored statistics (censored_stats — pulls NADA/NADA2), plots, Shiny. bw: domain DATA_CONTRACTS (catch/effort/fhap + new ones), analysis pipelines, explorers, process_qaqc domain rules. Anything needing NADA, EnvStats, shiny, ggplot2, statistical models.

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: Pure functions, side effects (except explicit profile read/write). Cold-session unit-testable — repos already hold line. Contracts data, passed — never hardcoded global. one substantive refactor vs bw’s current code: bw’s auto_map(source_cols, role) looks role global DATA_CONTRACTS. shared package functions take contract object (registry handle), repo owns contracts. thin compatibility shim bw (auto_map(cols, role) → auto_map(cols, DATA_CONTRACTS[[role]])) preserves current call sites. Lean dependencies. Ingestion . -built Imports readr, readxl, tibble, dplyr, tidyr, purrr, jsonlite plus base stats, tools, utils; arrow Suggests-(optional parquet cache backend). originally-floated rlang, stringr, , lubridate proved unnecessary dropped. stats/plot/Shiny stack, import-consumer stays light. Fuzzy matching uses base utils::adist (bw already ) — stringdist dependency. Backward-compatible migration. repo keeps current public function names thin re-exports/aliases shared ones, downstream call site breaks migration.

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: contract just list field specs (name, type ∈ character/numeric/ integer/date, required, synonyms, description). consuming repo holds named registry (bw’s DATA_CONTRACTS; wq’s new wq_contract()); tritonIngest never sees names.
code: contract <- as_contract(list(   cf_field("year",      "integer",   required = TRUE,  synonyms = c("yr", "sample_year")),   cf_field("site",      "character", required = TRUE,  synonyms = c("station", "site_id")),   cf_field("length_mm", "numeric",   required = TRUE,  synonyms = c("fork_length", "fl_mm")) ))

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
code: raw     <- read_tabular("2025 KO Spawner Raw Data.xlsx", sheet = "Data",                         col_names = FALSE)            # read header-less... tidy    <- clean_table(raw)                           # ...then recover header, drop blanks layout  <- detect_layout(tidy)$layout                 # "long" | "wide" long    <- if (layout == "wide") melt_wide(tidy, param_cols = ...) else tidy mapping <- auto_map(names(long), contract)            # best-guess col -> field mapped  <- apply_column_map(long, mapping, contract)  # rename/select/coerce report  <- validate_against_contract(mapped, contract) # chemistry only: mapped$detection_limit <- parse_censored(mapped$value_raw)$detection_limit mapped$working <- working_values(mapped$value, mapped$censored, mapped$detection_limit)

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
code: tritonIngest/   DESCRIPTION            # Imports: readr, readxl, tibble, dplyr, tidyr, purrr,                          #          jsonlite, stats, tools, utils                          # Suggests: arrow (parquet cache backend), testthat   NAMESPACE              # generated by roxygen   R/     read.R               # read_tabular, coerce_excel_date     clean.R              # find_header_row, clean_table, drop_blank_rows,                          #   drop_blank_cols  (structural junk removal)     layout.R             # is_value_like, detect_layout, melt_wide     contract.R           # cf_field, as_contract, contract_fields, auto_map,                          #   apply_column_map, validate_against_contract,                          #   complete_to_contract, contract_is_ready     profiles.R           # save/load/list/delete_mapping_profile, mapping_profiles_dir     censored.R           # parse_censored, apply_substitution, working_values     units.R              # convert_units     validate.R           # check_required_columns, check_column_types,                          #   type_matches, check_no_na, validation_abort                          #   (generic validation kernel — added post-design)     cache.R              # cache_dir, write_cache, read_cache, cached_ingest                          #   (materialisation cache — added post-design)     utils.R              # shared internal helpers (e.g. `%||%`)   tests/testthat/        # ported from both repos' existing tests + new modules   man/                   # roxygen   .github/workflows/     # R-CMD-check.yaml (CI)   renv/ + renv.lock      # pinned dependency environment   README.md   DESIGN.md              # this file

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: phase ends repos’ full test suites green. Status v0.4.2 (2026-06-16): ✅ done · ⬜ started.

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: Create tritonIngest repo (DESCRIPTION, package skeleton, roxygen, testthat). GitHub: shepherd70/tritonIngest.

what: Phase 1 — port the engine + utilities (no consumer touched) ✅
title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: Move functions §2 tritonIngest, refactored take contract objects (role strings) parameterized profiles directory. Port relevant existing tests repos: - wq: test-io_import.R, test-censored*.R (parsing parts), unit tests. - bw: test-data_contract.R, test-mapping_profiles.R, test-coerce_excel_date.R. Deliverable: tritonIngest standalone-green. Zero changes wq bw yet.

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: standalone, package also grew two domain-agnostic capabilities original §2 scope, plus project infrastructure: - validate.R — generic validation kernel (check_* → failure messages + validation_abort) downstream packages (tritonmr, electrocpue, …). distinct validate_against_contract(), checks frame contract object; kernel lower-level check battery. - cache.R — materialisation cache post-ingestion canonical object (rds / parquet backends, source-fingerprint invalidation). - CI (R-CMD-check.yaml) pinned renv environment.

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: Add tritonIngest bw Imports (+ Remotes/renv git ref). auto_map(cols, role) → tritonIngest::auto_map(cols, DATA_CONTRACTS[[role]]) keep DATA_CONTRACTS (domain) local. mapping_profiles_dir() → call shared ::("data","processed","mapping_profiles"). Run bw’s suite + Shiny explorer smoke path.

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: Add tritonIngest wq Imports. Re-export read_wq_file→read_tabular, detect_layout, melt_wide, parse_censored, apply_substitution, working_values, convert_units tritonIngest (keep old names aliases). Re-express suggest_roles/assemble_wqdata/import_wq top wq_contract() + auto_map() + shared censored parsing. wqdata model, guideline engine, stats, plots, Shiny stay local. Run wq’s full suite (currently 315 tests).

what: Phase 4 — the payoff: chemistry ingestion in bw ⬜
title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: Define chemistry/metals contract bw (analyte, value, units, censored, detection_limit, sample keys). Ingest bw’s tissue-metals + sediment workbooks tritonIngest’s non-detect parsing + unit reconciliation — capability bw lacks today.

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: New public/private GitHub repo shepherd70/tritonIngest, semver tags (v0.1.0 …). Consumers pin : DESCRIPTION Remotes: github::shepherd70/tritonIngest@v0.4.2 (current release) resolved ref recorded repo’s renv.lock. Breaking changes shared API bump minor version repos pre-1.0; never force-push tags.

title: tritonIngest — Design Document
text: Repo visibility — public private GitHub? (affects install auth renv/CI) read_tabular return contract — keep wq’s “everything character” default (preserves <DL notation, lets parse_censored run), opt-typed read? (proposed: yes) Profiles location — parameterize per call (proposed) vs package option (options(tritonIngest.profiles_dir=))? fold process_qaqc’s generic bits , leave QC-rule logic consumers? (proposed: leave domain QC consumers; ingestion ) Minimum R version — match stricter two repos.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Travis Shepherd. Author, maintainer.

title: Authors and Citation
text: Shepherd T (2026). tritonIngest: Tabular Data Ingestion Toolkit. R package version 0.4.3, https://github.com/shepherd70/tritonIngest.
code: @Manual{,   title = {tritonIngest: Tabular Data Ingestion Toolkit},   author = {Travis Shepherd},   year = {2026},   note = {R package version 0.4.3},   url = {https://github.com/shepherd70/tritonIngest}, }

title: Tabular Data Ingestion Toolkit
text: Domain-agnostic R primitives ingesting messy field laboratory data workbooks. Shared plumbing water-chemistry-qaqc (chemistry) bw-analysis-code (fish / effort / habitat) projects don’t duplicate ingestion layers. contains domain knowledge, statistics, plotting — just reusable ingestion engine. See DESIGN.md rationale, full API, cross-repo migration plan.

title: Tabular Data Ingestion Toolkit
text: Read CSV/TSV/XLSX -text (read_tabular) fragile notation survives; coerce mixed Excel-serial/ISO dates (coerce_excel_date). Clean structural junk — find real header row strip blank rows / spacer columns workbook title metadata rows data (clean_table, find_header_row, drop_blank_rows, drop_blank_cols). Reshape — detect long vs wide layout (detect_layout) melt wide → long (melt_wide). Map schema — declare contract (cf_field + as_contract), auto-map source columns onto exact / synonym / fuzzy match (auto_map), apply + coerce (apply_column_map), validate (validate_against_contract), fill/complete (complete_to_contract, contract_is_ready). Reuse mappings — save/load named JSON column-mapping profiles (save_mapping_profile, load_mapping_profile, …). Lab values — parse non-detects (parse_censored), substitute (apply_substitution, working_values), reconcile units (convert_units). Validate (generic) — run battery domain-agnostic schema checks return failure messages, abort collected set (check_required_columns, check_column_types, check_no_na, validation_abort). Domain rules stay consuming packages. Cache — materialise parsed canonical object fast-reload cache keyed fingerprint source file, unchanged source skips re-ingestion moved source auto-invalidates (write_cache, read_cache, cached_ingest; rds parquet backend).

title: Tabular Data Ingestion Toolkit
Tabular Data Ingestion Toolkit
code: library(tritonIngest)  # read header-less so title/metadata rows above the header can be stripped raw   <- read_tabular("workbook.xlsx", sheet = "Data", col_names = FALSE) tidy  <- clean_table(raw)                         # find header, drop blank rows/cols long  <- if (detect_layout(tidy)$layout == "wide")            melt_wide(tidy, param_cols = c("Zinc", "Copper")) else tidy  # declare what the analysis needs (each project keeps its own contracts) chem <- as_contract(list(   cf_field("site",      "character", required = TRUE,  synonyms = "station"),   cf_field("parameter", "character", required = TRUE,  synonyms = "analyte"),   cf_field("value_raw", "character", required = TRUE) ))  mapping <- auto_map(names(long), chem)            # best-guess column -> field mapped  <- apply_column_map(long, mapping, chem)  # rename + coerce report  <- validate_against_contract(mapped, chem) parsed  <- parse_censored(mapped$value_raw)       # non-detects -> value/censored/DL

title: Tabular Data Ingestion Toolkit
text: v0.4.3 — current release (R CMD check ---cran clean v0.4.2; v0.4.3 adds plural-column-name recognition whitespace trimming layout detection). Standalone package, ported tested, R-CMD-check CI workflow renv lockfile. Beyond original Phase 0–1 engine also ships generic validation kernel (validate.R) materialisation cache (cache.R). Migration consumer repos (DESIGN.md §5, Phases 2–4) started yet.

what: Apply a column mapping to a source data frame. — apply_column_map
title: Apply a column mapping to a source data frame. — apply_column_map
text: Selects mapped source columns, renames contract field names, (optionally) coerces declared type. Fields whose mapping NA/missing dropped — use validate_against_contract() afterwards flag missing required fields. Unreferenced source columns discarded, downstream code sees contract-named columns.

title: Apply a column mapping to a source data frame. — apply_column_map
Apply a column mapping to a source data frame. — apply_column_map
text: df source data frame. mapping Named list/character vector: contract field -> source column. contract contract (list specs tibble). coerce Logical; coerce output column declared type.

title: Apply a column mapping to a source data frame. — apply_column_map
text: tibble contract-named (subset ) columns.

what: Apply a simple substitution rule to censored values. — apply_substitution
title: Apply a simple substitution rule to censored values. — apply_substitution
text: Returns numeric vector censored entries replaced fraction * detection_limit; detected entries pass unchanged. Transparent widely used, can bias variance estimates.

title: Apply a simple substitution rule to censored values. — apply_substitution
Apply a simple substitution rule to censored values. — apply_substitution
text: value Numeric vector (NA censored). censored Logical vector. detection_limit Numeric vector DLs. fraction Substitution fraction: 0.5 (default, = 1/2 DL), 1 (DL), 0.

title: Apply a simple substitution rule to censored values. — apply_substitution
text: Numeric vector censored entries substituted (NA DL unknown).

what: Coerce field specs into a contract tibble. — as_contract
title: Coerce field specs into a contract tibble. — as_contract
text: Accepts list cf_field() specs, already-built contract tibble (idempotent), engine functions can take either form.

title: Coerce field specs into a contract tibble. — as_contract
Coerce field specs into a contract tibble. — as_contract
text: x list cf_field() specs, contract tibble.

title: Coerce field specs into a contract tibble. — as_contract
text: tibble columns field, type, required, synonyms (list-column), description.

what: Auto-map source columns onto a contract. — auto_map
title: Auto-map source columns onto a contract. — auto_map
text: contract field, picks best source column : (1) normalised exact match field name, (2) normalised match field's synonyms, (3) fuzzy match (utils::adist) within small edit-distance budget. source column used ; earlier (higher-priority) fields win ties.

title: Auto-map source columns onto a contract. — auto_map
Auto-map source columns onto a contract. — auto_map
text: source_cols Character vector column names source data. contract contract (list specs tibble). max_distance Integer max edit distance fuzzy fallback; set 0 disable fuzzy matching.

title: Auto-map source columns onto a contract. — auto_map
text: named list, one element per contract field, holding matched source-column name NA_character_.

what: Resolve (and optionally create) the cache directory. — cache_dir
title: Resolve (and optionally create) the cache directory. — cache_dir
text: Resolve (optionally create) cache directory.

title: Resolve (and optionally create) the cache directory. — cache_dir
Resolve (and optionally create) the cache directory. — cache_dir
text: dir Directory store cache files . Defaults getOption("tritonIngest.cache_dir"); errors neither set. create Logical; create directory exist.

title: Resolve (and optionally create) the cache directory. — cache_dir
text: Absolute path cache directory.

what: Ingest a source, using the cache when fresh and rebuilding it when not. — cached_ingest
title: Ingest a source, using the cache when fresh and rebuilding it when not. — cached_ingest
text: standard ingest--cache flow: try read_cache(); miss/stale source, run parse(source, ...), write result cache, return . parse defaults read_tabular() typically project-specific function parses validates canonical object.

title: Ingest a source, using the cache when fresh and rebuilding it when not. — cached_ingest
code: cached_ingest(   source,   parse = read_tabular,   key = NULL,   dir = getOption("tritonIngest.cache_dir"),   format = c("rds", "parquet"),   fingerprint = c("md5", "size_mtime"),   ... )

title: Ingest a source, using the cache when fresh and rebuilding it when not. — cached_ingest
text: source Path(s) source file(s). parse Function called parse(source, ...) cache miss; must return object cache. Defaults read_tabular(). key Cache key. Defaults one derived source. dir Cache directory (see cache_dir()). format "rds" (default) "parquet". fingerprint Source-fingerprint method (see write_cache()). ... Passed parse.

title: Ingest a source, using the cache when fresh and rebuilding it when not. — cached_ingest
text: canonical object, cache freshly parsed.

title: Build one contract field specification. — cf_field
text: Build one contract field specification.

title: Build one contract field specification. — cf_field
code: cf_field(   name,   type = c("character", "numeric", "integer", "date"),   required = FALSE,   synonyms = character(0),   description = "" )

title: Build one contract field specification. — cf_field
text: name Contract field name (canonical output column name). type One "character", "numeric", "integer", "date". required Logical; field required data usable? synonyms Character vector alternative source-column names map field (matched name normalisation). description Short human-readable description.

what: Clean a freshly-read table: promote the header and strip junk. — clean_table
title: Clean a freshly-read table: promote the header and strip junk. — clean_table
text: Locates header row (uses header_row), makes column names, drops everything , removes fully-blank rows columns optionally trims whitespace. Cell values never coerced – -text contract read_tabular() preserved.

Clean a freshly-read table: promote the header and strip junk. — clean_table
text: df data frame -text, header-less rows (see read_tabular()). header_row Optional integer: force row header instead detecting . trim_ws Trim leading/trailing whitespace header cell text.

title: Clean a freshly-read table: promote the header and strip junk. — clean_table
text: tibble promoted names structural junk removed.

title: Clean a freshly-read table: promote the header and strip junk. — clean_table
text: Expects header-less frame, .e. read col_names = FALSE real header still data row found. Blank duplicated header names filled (col_<position>) made unique rather silently collapsed. fully blank rows/columns dropped (see note clean.R).

what: Coerce mixed Excel-serial / ISO-string dates to Date. — coerce_excel_date
title: Coerce mixed Excel-serial / ISO-string dates to Date. — coerce_excel_date
text: Worksheet date columns ingested Excel sometimes arrive mix Excel serial numbers (e.g. "45909" 45909) ISO date strings (e.g. "2023-08-22"), depending cell formatted. naive .Date() mangles serials naive serial conversion drops ISO strings NA. detects element's encoding coerces single Date vector (1900 date system, origin 1899-12-30), leaving genuinely missing/unparseable values NA.

title: Coerce mixed Excel-serial / ISO-string dates to Date. — coerce_excel_date
Coerce mixed Excel-serial / ISO-string dates to Date. — coerce_excel_date
text: x vector (numeric character) Excel-serial numbers /ISO "YYYY-MM-DD" strings.

title: Coerce mixed Excel-serial / ISO-string dates to Date. — coerce_excel_date
text: Date vector length x.

title: Complete a mapped frame to the full contract schema. — complete_to_contract

what: Heuristically detect whether a table is long/tidy or wide. — detect_layout
title: Heuristically detect whether a table is long/tidy or wide. — detect_layout
text: Long signals: parameter-ish column name value-ish column name, single value-like column. Wide signals: two value-like columns (variables spread across columns). caller can override result.

title: Heuristically detect whether a table is long/tidy or wide. — detect_layout
code: detect_layout(   df,   param_names = c("parameter", "parameters", "param", "params", "analyte", "analytes",     "characteristic", "characteristics", "characteristicname", "variable", "variables",     "constituent", "constituents"),   value_names = c("value", "values", "result", "results", "resultvalue", "value_raw",     "concentration", "concentrations", "conc", "measurement", "measurements") )

title: Heuristically detect whether a table is long/tidy or wide. — detect_layout
text: df data frame (typically read -text via read_tabular()). param_names, value_names Character vectors lowercase column names signal long-format parameter / value column. Matched lowercasing whitespace-trimming data's names. Override match domain's vocabulary.

title: Heuristically detect whether a table is long/tidy or wide. — detect_layout
text: list: layout ("long"/"wide"), value_like_cols, reason.

title: Heuristically detect whether a table is long/tidy or wide. — detect_layout
text: Column names matched case-insensitively trimming surrounding whitespace, header exported "Analyte " (trailing space common lab reports) still hits vocabulary. default vocabularies carry singular plural forms ("result"/"results", "analyte"/"analytes", ...) labs inconsistent: ALS export, instance, labels value column "Results". Missing plural drop long signal , two numeric columns present (value + detection limit + numeric QC-lot id), misclassify table wide.

text: Given frame read every row data (read_tabular(path, col_names = FALSE)), guess row holds column names. Leading blank rows sparse title/metadata rows skipped; header taken first well-populated row whose cells look like names rather values, data beneath . Reuses is_value_like() tell names values.

title: Melt a wide table (variables as columns) to long form. — melt_wide
code: melt_wide(   df,   param_cols,   id_cols = setdiff(names(df), param_cols),   units = NULL )

title: Melt a wide table (variables as columns) to long form. — melt_wide
text: df Wide data frame. param_cols Character vector measured-variable column names melt. id_cols Columns keep identifiers; default everything else. units Optional named character vector mapping parameter -> units, since wide files usually carry units header codebook rather per cell. Unmatched parameters get NA units.

title: Melt a wide table (variables as columns) to long form. — melt_wide
text: Long tibble columns: id_cols..., parameter, value_raw, units.

Parse raw result text into value / censored / detection_limit. — parse_censored
text: value_raw Character vector raw results read file. detection_limit Optional numeric vector (recycled length 1) separate DL/RL column; used token non-detects ("ND") checked consistency "<DL" notation.

title: Parse raw result text into value / censored / detection_limit. — parse_censored
text: tibble: value (dbl, NA censored/unparseable), censored (lgl, NA unparseable), detection_limit (dbl), parse_note (chr, NA clean).

what: Read from the materialisation cache, if present and still fresh. — read_cache
title: Read from the materialisation cache, if present and still fresh. — read_cache
text: Returns cached object data file sidecar exist — sidecar recorded source fingerprint — current source still matches . miss stale/changed source returns NULL (message), caller re-parses rather trusting --date cache.

title: Read from the materialisation cache, if present and still fresh. — read_cache
code: read_cache(   key = NULL,   source = NULL,   dir = getOption("tritonIngest.cache_dir"),   format = c("rds", "parquet") )

title: Read from the materialisation cache, if present and still fresh. — read_cache
text: key Cache key. Defaults one derived source. source Path(s) current source file(s); compared recorded fingerprint. NULL skips freshness check. dir Cache directory (see cache_dir()). format "rds" (default) "parquet".

title: Read from the materialisation cache, if present and still fresh. — read_cache
text: cached object, NULL miss / stale source.

what: Read a tabular data file (CSV/TSV/XLSX) as all-text columns. — read_tabular
title: Read a tabular data file (CSV/TSV/XLSX) as all-text columns. — read_tabular
text: Every column read character notation like non-detect markers ("<0.01", "ND") codes leading zeros preserved downstream parsing. Pass col_types override typed read needed.

title: Read a tabular data file (CSV/TSV/XLSX) as all-text columns. — read_tabular
code: read_tabular(path, sheet = NULL, col_types = NULL, col_names = TRUE)

title: Read a tabular data file (CSV/TSV/XLSX) as all-text columns. — read_tabular
text: path File path. extension selects reader (csv/txt, tsv, xlsx/xls). sheet Sheet name index Excel files (default: first sheet). col_types Optional column-type spec passed underlying reader; default reads everything text. col_names Treat first row header (TRUE, default) read every row data positional names (FALSE). Use FALSE workbook title/metadata rows real header, recover clean_table().

title: Read a tabular data file (CSV/TSV/XLSX) as all-text columns. — read_tabular
text: tibble.

what: Check whether an actual R class satisfies an expected type spec — type_matches
title: Check whether an actual R class satisfies an expected type spec — type_matches
text: "numeric" accepts numeric, double, integer; "integer" strict; "Date" (contract-style lowercase "date") requires Date class; "logical" "character" exact. spec falls back exact class match.

title: Check whether an actual R class satisfies an expected type spec — type_matches
Check whether an actual R class satisfies an expected type spec — type_matches
text: actual First element class() column checked. expected Expected type spec string.

title: Check whether an actual R class satisfies an expected type spec — type_matches
text: Logical scalar.

what: Validate a (mapped) data frame against a contract. — validate_against_contract
title: Validate a (mapped) data frame against a contract. — validate_against_contract
text: Reports, per contract field, whether present usable. Statuses: "ok", "missing" (column absent), "all_na" (present every value NA), "type_warn" (declared numeric/integer >50% non-coercible).

title: Validate a (mapped) data frame against a contract. — validate_against_contract
Validate a (mapped) data frame against a contract. — validate_against_contract
text: df data frame contract-named columns. contract contract (list specs tibble).

title: Validate a (mapped) data frame against a contract. — validate_against_contract
text: tibble: field, required, status, severity ("error"/"warning"/"ok"), issue (NA ok).

what: Abort with a classed error listing all collected validation failures — validation_abort
title: Abort with a classed error listing all collected validation failures — validation_abort
text: Standardizes collect--failures--abort pattern: run every check, concatenate returned failure messages, call . nothing failures empty, can called unconditionally end validator.

title: Abort with a classed error listing all collected validation failures — validation_abort
Abort with a classed error listing all collected validation failures — validation_abort
text: failures Character vector failure messages (possibly empty). class Additional condition class error, prepended "triton_validation_error" callers can keep package-specific classes (e.g. "cpue_validation_error"). header Optional header line; defaults count issues.

title: Abort with a classed error listing all collected validation failures — validation_abort
text: Invisibly TRUE failures empty; otherwise signals error class c(class, "triton_validation_error") whose failures field holds full message vector.

what: Working numeric values for a censoring method. — working_values
title: Working numeric values for a censoring method. — working_values
text: single censoring "switch" code needs plain numeric vector. Maps (value, censored, detection_limit) working values chosen non-detect handling. substitution supported ; robust group-level estimators (KM/ROS) live consuming package.

title: Working numeric values for a censoring method. — working_values
code: working_values(   value,   censored,   detection_limit,   method = c("substitution"),   fraction = 0.5 )

title: Working numeric values for a censoring method. — working_values
text: value Numeric vector (NA censored). censored Logical vector (NA = unparseable). detection_limit Numeric vector DLs. method Censoring method; currently "substitution". fraction Substitution fraction (0, 0.5, 1).

title: Working numeric values for a censoring method. — working_values
text: Numeric vector censored entries handled per method.

what: Write a parsed object to the materialisation cache. — write_cache
title: Write a parsed object to the materialisation cache. — write_cache
text: Writes data file plus JSON sidecar recording source fingerprint, format, timestamp shape, read_cache() can later decide whether cache still fresh.

title: Write a parsed object to the materialisation cache. — write_cache
code: write_cache(   x,   source = NULL,   key = NULL,   dir = getOption("tritonIngest.cache_dir"),   format = c("rds", "parquet"),   fingerprint = c("md5", "size_mtime"),   meta = list() )

title: Write a parsed object to the materialisation cache. — write_cache
text: x object cache. format = "parquet" must data frame; classes/attributes beyond plain table dropped (warning) — use "rds" preserve classed object exactly. source Path(s) source file(s) object parsed . Used fingerprint inputs; may NULL cache without invalidation. key Cache key (file stem). Defaults one derived source. dir Cache directory (see cache_dir()). format "rds" (default) "parquet". fingerprint Source-fingerprint method, "md5" (default) "size_mtime" (cheaper large sources). meta Optional named list extra provenance record sidecar.

title: Write a parsed object to the materialisation cache. — write_cache
text: path written data file (invisibly).

title: tritonIngest 0.4.3
text: detect_layout() now recognises plural parameter/value column names ("results", "values", "analytes", "parameters", …) trims surrounding whitespace column names matching. Lab exports ALS reports label value column "Results" pad headers ("Analyte "), previously missed long-format vocabulary – two numeric columns present (result + detection limit + numeric QC-lot id) – misclassified table wide, discarding analyte column. removes need interim water-chemistry-qaqc guard.
